chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1793 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders as to maintainability of petition. 14.05.2025 Mr. Khursheed Ahmed Khan is present on behalf of petitioner. On the last date, following office objections were recorded and petitioner was given a further opportunity to address the same: “1. How this Petition is maintainable under the Regular Bench Matter, as the matter is falling under the ambit of writ of Mandamus, which is falling under the ambit of Constitutional Bench? 2. How this petition is maintainable against the Respondent No.02 in view of Article 248 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973? 3. How this petition is maintainable before this Honourable Court, as all the respondents are outside of the Sindh, and bases in Lahore, Islamabad, Rawalpindi except Respondent No.8? 4. How this petition is maintainable without exhausting remedy before the concerned authorities? 5. Address of Respondents No.3 & 7 to be given with their names. 6. Why separate case was not filed for different relief or different department? 7. Why other HGOs are not filing their cases for their individual rights? 8. How the Petitioner is an aggrieved person with regard to other HGOs.? Today, once again he remains unable to satisfy the court on the count of maintainability. Petition is found to be mis-conceived, hence, dismissed in limine. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
